title: The Greek Alphabet
description: Learn the 24 letters of the Greek alphabet, their names, and basic pronunciation.
learningObjectives: Understand the relationship between Greek and English letters
Write Greek letters in both uppercase and lowercase

sections.title: Introduction to the Greek Alphabet
sections.content:
The Greek alphabet is the ancestor of many modern alphabets, including the Latin alphabet used in English. It consists of 24 letters, each with both uppercase and lowercase forms.

The Greek alphabet was developed around 800 BCE and has been used continuously since then. It's the foundation for learning Ancient Greek, as every word in the language is written using these letters.

examples.explanation: The first letter of the Greek alphabet, similar to English 'A'

sections.title: Uppercase Letters
sections.content: Greek uppercase letters are used at the beginning of sentences, proper nouns, and for emphasis. Here are all 24 uppercase letters:

examples.greek: Α Β Γ Δ Ε Ζ Η Θ Ι Κ Λ Μ Ν Ξ Ο Π Ρ Σ Τ Υ Φ Χ Ψ Ω
examples.translation: Alpha, Beta, Gamma, Delta, Epsilon, Zeta, Eta, Theta, Iota, Kappa, Lambda, Mu, Nu, Xi, Omicron, Pi, Rho, Sigma, Tau, Upsilon, Phi, Chi, Psi, Omega
examples.pronunciation: ah-lfah, bay-tah, gah-mah, del-tah, ep-si-lon, zay-tah, ay-tah, thay-tah, ee-oh-tah, kah-pah, lahm-dah, moo, noo, ksee, oh-mee-kron, pee, roh, sig-mah, tow, oop-si-lon, fee, khee, psee, oh-may-gah

sections.title: Lowercase Letters
sections.content: Greek lowercase letters are used for most writing. Here are all 24 lowercase letters:

examples.greek: α β γ δ ε ζ η θ ι κ λ μ ν ξ ο π ρ σ τ υ φ χ ψ ω
examples.translation: alpha, beta, gamma, delta, epsilon, zeta, eta, theta, iota, kappa, lambda, mu, nu, xi, omicron, pi, rho, sigma, tau, upsilon, phi, chi, psi, omega
examples.pronunciation: ah-lfah, bay-tah, gah-mah, del-tah, ep-si-lon, zay-tah, ay-tah, thay-tah, ee-oh-tah, kah-pah, lahm-dah, moo, noo, ksee, oh-mee-kron, pee, roh, sig-mah, tow, oop-si-lon, fee, khee, psee, oh-may-gah

sections.title: Pronunciation Guide
sections.content:
Here are some important pronunciation notes for Greek letters:

  • Most Greek letters have similar sounds to their English counterparts
  • Some letters have unique sounds not found in English
  • Accent marks can change pronunciation
  • Ancient Greek pronunciation differs from modern Greek

examples.explanation: Like 'th' in 'think' - this sound doesn't exist in modern Greek

sections.title: Letter Recognition Practice
sections.content: Practice recognizing Greek letters. Match each Greek letter with its English name.

questions.question: What is the name of the Greek letter Α?
questions.explanation: Α is called Alpha, the first letter of the Greek alphabet.

questions.question: What is the name of the Greek letter Ω?
questions.explanation: Ω is called Omega, the last letter of the Greek alphabet.

questions.question: Which Greek letter makes the 'th' sound?
questions.explanation: Θ (Theta) makes the 'th' sound as in 'think'.

exercises.title: Greek Alphabet Quiz
exercises.description: Test your knowledge of the Greek alphabet

questions.question: What is the first letter of the Greek alphabet?
questions.explanation: Α (Alpha) is the first letter of the Greek alphabet.

questions.question: What is the last letter of the Greek alphabet?
questions.explanation: Ω (Omega) is the last letter of the Greek alphabet.

questions.question: How many letters are in the Greek alphabet?

exercises.instructions: Answer all questions about the Greek alphabet. You have 10 minutes to complete this quiz.
